page: Microsoft Azure A8m_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine belonging to the Av2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8171M processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is limited to an 82 GB volume, and there are no integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate that the A8m_v2 exhibits lower-tier processing and memory performance. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores fall into the bottom 10% tier, while multi-core metrics from stress-ng and Geekbench rank in the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth tests show read-only large block speeds of 32.02 GB/sec and write speeds of 24.79 GB/sec, placing memory performance in the bottom 10% to 25% categories. Cryptographic and compression tasks, such as bzip3 decompression and Passmark encryption, also demonstrate weak to poor throughput.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the A8m_v2 offers a high memory capacity relative to its processing power, making it a budget-friendly option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high-performance compute. It is suitable for relational databases with moderate transaction volumes, basic caching layers, and general-purpose applications requiring a larger memory footprint without intensive CPU demands. It is less suited for high-throughput web serving, heavy compilation, or machine learning tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure A8m_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine from the Av2 family, utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with 8 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8171M processor running at 2.6 GHz, 64.0 GB of RAM, and 82 GB of local storage. The instance does not include a GPU. Performance benchmarks place this server in the lower tiers, with single-core and multi-core CPU metrics ranking in the bottom 10% and 25% respectively, and memory bandwidth showing similar constraints. Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio but modest processing capabilities, the A8m_v2 is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-heavy workloads with low CPU demands. It is best suited for moderate database operations, basic caching, and general-purpose applications requiring a larger memory footprint.
og_description: Microsoft Azure A8m_v2 is an Av2-family virtual machine featuring 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and 82 GB of local storage, designed for memory-intensive workloads with moderate CPU requirements.

tagline: An x86 virtual machine offering a high memory-to-core ratio and local storage for memory-intensive workloads with moderate processing requirements.

64.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
82 GB of local storage with no integrated GPU
Best suited for moderate databases and caching layers
